questions.question:
In the slow start phase of the TCP congestion algorithm, the size of the congestion window:



GATE CSE 2008 | Question: 56

Consider an instance of TCP’s Additive Increase Multiplicative Decrease (AIMD) algorithm where the window size at the start of the slow start phase is \( 2 \) MSS and the threshold at the start of the first transmission is \( 8 \) MSS. Assume that a timeout occurs during the fifth transmission. Find the congestion window size at the end of the tenth transmission.



GATE CSE 2012 | Question: 45

Let the size of congestion window of a TCP connection be \( 32 \) KB when a timeout occurs. The round trip time of the connection is \( 100 \) msec and the maximum segment size used is \( 2 \) KB. The time taken (in msec) by the TCP connection to get back to \( 32 \) KB congestion window is _________.



GATE CSE 2014 Set 1 | Question: 27

Consider a LAN with four nodes \( S_1, S_2, S_3, \) and \( S_4 \). Time is divided into fixed-size slots, and a node can begin its transmission only at the beginning of a slot. A collision is said to have occurred if more than one node transmits in the same slot. The probabilities of generation of a frame in a time slot by \( S_1, S_2, S_3, \) and \( S_4 \) are \( 0.1, 0.2, 0.3 \) and \( 0.4 \) respectively. The probability of sending a frame in the first slot without any collision by any of these four stations is__________________.


GATE CSE 2015 Set 1 | Question: 29

Consider the following statements regarding the slow start phase of the TCP congestion control algorithm. Note that cwnd stands for the TCP congestion window and MSS window denotes the Maximum Segments Size:

  1. The cwnd increases by \( 2 \) MSS on every successful acknowledgment
  2. The cwnd approximately doubles on every successful acknowledgment
  3. The cwnd increases by \( 1 \) MSS every round trip time
  4. The cwnd approximately doubles every round trip time

Which one of the following is correct?



GATE CSE 2018 | Question: 14

On a TCP connection, current congestion window size is Congestion Window = \( 4 \) KB. The window size advertised by the receiver is Advertise Window = \( 6 \) KB. The last byte sent by the sender is LastByteSent = \( 10240 \) and the last byte acknowledged by the receiver is LastByteAcked = \( 8192 \). The current window size at the sender is:



GATE IT 2005 | Question: 73
